tasks: Clean desk (move one item, declare victory)
Watch 3 hours of YouTube 'for research'
Reorganize Downloads folder
Learn Icelandic
Alphabetize the spice rack
Read every 1-star review of a product you'll never buy
Rename all your variables to be 2% clearer
Reconsider your entire career over coffee
Update software that was working fine
Find the perfect lo-fi playlist (search will take 90 minutes)
Refactor code that nobody asked you to touch
Make a to-do list about making to-do lists
Stare meaningfully out the nearest window
Google your own symptoms for fun
Reply to that email from 2019
Adopt a new keyboard layout for productivity
Reach inbox zero, achieve nothing
Water a plant that is, regrettably, plastic
Tweak your terminal theme for the seventh time
Read the entire changelog of a library you don't use
Organize your bookmarks into folders you'll never open
Watch a documentary about productivity
Sharpen pencils you do not own
Plan an elaborate trip you will not take
Calculate how much sleep you'd get if you slept right now
Test whether your microphone works (it does)
Reinstall your OS 'for a fresh start'
Argue with a stranger on the internet, lose, learn nothing
Reorganize the apps on your phone by color
Practice your acceptance speech for an award you'll never win
Look up the lore of a video game you don't play
Deep-clean a keyboard one key at a time
Schedule a meeting to discuss the work instead of doing it
Compare 14 note-taking apps, use none
Read about the productivity habits of dead philosophers
closers: Good luck avoiding your actual task.
Your real work will still be here. It's patient like that.
Productivity is just procrastination with better marketing.
Remember: a busy day and a productive day are not the same thing.
The task you're avoiding sends its regards.
You've got this. 'This' being everything except the thing.
Procrastination is the art of keeping up with yesterday.
